{"query":"Ukraine Russia peace deal current status terms signing timeline 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1617,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peace_negotiations_in_the_Russo-Ukrainian_war_(2022%E2%80%93present)","title":"Peace negotiations in the Russo-Ukrainian war (2022–present) - Wikipedia","content":"Renewed negotiations began in 2025 after Donald Trump became president of the United States.
Early negotiations (February–April 2022)
On 20 March, Turkey's Foreign Minister Mevlüt Çavuşoğlu described the negotiations as "making progress".
Further peace talks between Russia and Ukraine began in Istanbul on 29 March 2022.
Ahead of the 29 March meeting, Turkish president Recep Tayyip Erdoğan said Ukraine was willing to agree to 4 out of Russia's 6 demands.
On 13 May 2022, US secretary of defense Lloyd Austin initiated a telephone conversation with Russian minister of defense Sergei Shoigu, the first call since 18 February, before the invasion.
Putin's peace terms and ceasefire proposals (October 2023–June 2024)
Trump assumed office as US president on 20 January 2025.
On 19 April 2025, Putin announced Russia's intention to implement a 30-hour truce (from 6:00 p.m.
Trilateral meetings between the US, Ukraine and Russia took place in Geneva, Switzerland, on 17 and 18 February 2026.
43% supported peace negotiations with Russia, while 54% were against.
65% opposed freezing the war at the current frontline, while 22% were in favor.
According to our military analyst Michael Clarke, Russia has broken 190 deals.
"Tucker Carlson's Putin interview: 9 takeaways".

Order of battle: The Russian military may have formed a new artillery regiment under the 20th Motorized Rifle Division.
1400 16th Street NW, Suite 515"},{"url":"https://legis1.com/news/ukraine-peace-negotiations-diplomatic-path-to","title":"Ukraine Peace Talks: Diplomatic Path Rapidly Narrowing | Legis1","content":"Stalled talks, competing demands from both sides, and the ripple effects of U.S. military operations in the Middle East are among the reasons.
Building on talks that began in 2025, Russian, Ukrainian, and U.S. officials met for the first time in a trilateral format in Abu Dhabi in January 2026.
Formal negotiations between Russia and Ukraine have not resumed since U.S.-Israeli military operations against Iran began in late February 2026, according to the report.
Some NATO and European officials have reportedly expressed concern that U.S. military operations against Iran could lead to procurement shortages and delays that affect assistance to Ukraine, adding another layer of uncertainty to an already strained supply picture.
Ukraine, for its part, has pursued new defense cooperation agreements of its own, signing arrangements with Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, and Germany, with a particular focus on drone and anti-drone warfare capabilities.
The Trump Administration has staked significant political capital on its ability to broker a deal that ends the war."},{"url":"https://www.nato.int/en/about-us/official-texts-and-resources/official-texts/2026/07/08/the-ankara-summit-declaration","title":"The Ankara Summit Declaration | NATO Official text","content":"In 2025, European Allies and Canada increased their investments in core defence requirements by more than $139 billion.
For 2026, Allies pledge €70 billion in military equipment, assistance and training for Ukraine and affirm their sovereign commitments to sustaining at least equivalent levels in 2027."}]}

{"query":"Ukraine Russia peace deal current status terms signing timeline 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=71M0jqNx9Fw","title":"Trump sets June deadline for Ukraine-Russia peace | FOX 5 News","content":"Trump sets June deadline for Ukraine-Russia peace | FOX 5 News
FOX 5 Atlanta
713000 subscribers

9 Feb 2026
The Trump administration has issued a strict June 2026 deadline for Ukraine and Russia to reach a comprehensive peace agreement, warning that both nations will face "significant pressure" if the timeline is missed. U.S. officials have proposed moving the next round of trilateral negotiations to Miami, Florida, which would mark the first time such talks have occurred on U.S. soil. The diplomatic push comes as Russia launched a massive aerial campaign against Ukraine's energy grid, forcing nuclear power plants to reduce output and drawing sharp criticism from lawmakers who view the strikes as a stall tactic.

Volodymyr Zelenskyy has said the US has given Ukraine and Russia yet another deadline to reach a peace settlement, and is now proposing the war should end by June. Zelenskyy said the Trump administration “will probably put pressure” on Ukraine and Russia to end the war by the beginning of the summer. Firstpost

6 Mar 2026
Four years into the Russia–Ukraine war, the path to peace remains uncertain. Ex- Ukrainian Foreign Minister Pavlo Klimkin explains the major obstacles preventing a peace agreement with Russia. According to him, the conflict goes beyond territory or political disputes. He argues that Russia refuses to recognise Ukraine’s identity, language, history and statehood, viewing the country as part of its own narrative of a “Russian world”. This fundamental disagreement makes compromise extremely difficult. While Ukraine may consider practical negotiations, it cannot surrender its sovereignty or national existence. Until this core issue is addressed, he believes a lasting peace between the two sides will remain out of reach.
